channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Can You Get Shiny Mimikyu in Pokémon Go?
item.content:encoded:
Pokémon Go fans have a new Pokémon to track down on April Fool’s Day and during the A Shockingly Good Time Go Pass period in the game, with Mimikyu debuting, which naturally raises questions about Shiny Mimikyu’s availability.

Shiny hunting is an extremely popular pastime in the Pokémon community, and Pokémon Go has made it easier than ever to Shiny hunt by making various Pokémon available while out and about.

However, not every Pokémon available in Pokémon Go has its Shiny form available to encounter, and Insider Gaming has all the details on Shiny Mimikyu’s availability.

Can You Catch Shiny Mimikyu in Pokémon Go?

No, Shiny Mimikyu is not available in Pokémon Go despite the addition of the disguise Pokémon on April Fool’s Day.

While the fan-favorite is available for a limited time on April Fool’s Day, and as a reward in the A Shockingly Good Time Go Pass, Shiny Mimikyu is not in the encounter pool.

  • More Insider Gaming: How to Get Mimikyu in Pokémon Go

During the event and April Fool’s Day, however, there are a host of Pokémon that can be encountered as their Shiny form, including Shiny Pikachu during the Pikachu Spotlight Hour between 7 PM and 8 PM local time on April 1.

For anyone wanting a Shiny Mimikyu, however, it could be a lengthy wait for its addition to Pokémon Go, as Niantic tends to scatter the drops.

When Will Shiny Mimikyu be Added to Pokémon Go?

There are no details on when Shiny Mimikyu will be available in Pokémon Go, and the wait for the special form to be introduced by Niantic could be a long one.

Niantic has spread out the drops of new Pokémon and the debut of their Shiny forms throughout the 10-year history of the game, often with huge gaps between them.

For example, the Shiny forms of Scorbunny, Raboot, and Cinderace were added to Pokémon Go on March 14 as part of the March 2026 Community Day, yet the trio made their debuts in September 2024.

  • More Insider Gaming: Is Pokémon Champions on Nintendo Switch & Mobile?

That huge span in time means it could be a long wait for Shiny Mimikyu to appear in Pokémon Go, particularly as Mimikyu itself is only available as a limited-time option.

Before a Shiny Mimikyu debut is even considered, Mimikyu needs to appear more regularly in Pokémon Go, but Niantic’s approach suggests it will be saved for special events, like Halloween.

When do you want to see Shiny Mimikyu debut in Pokémon Go? Share your thoughts in the Insider Gaming Discord.

For more Pokémon, find out about the April Fool’s Event in Pokopia, and take a look at the pricing details for Pokémon Champions.

MOBILE APP

item.title: “It’s hard to have a life next to Dota” – MOUZ Seleri ESL One Birmingham Full Interview
item.content:encoded:
Insider Gaming broke the news of Melchior ‘Seleri’ Hillenkamp’s decision to step away from competitive Dota 2, a decision which has since been confirmed by the player and the MOUZ organization.

Below is the full interview where that information was revealed, with Seleri reflecting on ESL One Birmingham 2026, which looks to be the legend’s final event, at least for now.

You left the event vs Xtreme Gaming. How are you feeling about that match?

Ah, I’m feeling fine. It was a bit annoying, because I really wanted to play on stage. So, we only got to play once.

But it also feels like we tried our best, and the patch didn’t help us, to be honest. We just did our best. So, I’m happy about that.

Game 1, you were fully in control. Do you think it was just the draft in Game 2 and 3, or do you think there’s something deeper going on?

I mean, it’s nice to just blame it on the draft. But in the first game, we talked about it. Our coach also told us that it wasn’t easy to play with our draft.

We needed to burst them, and we needed to not get too far behind from the lanes, but it’s very hard. They had two heroes with saves. So, we tried to burst, but there’s always one guy with a save there. But in the second game, we can definitely win.

In the end, you win or you lose as a team. It’s not like it’s one person’s fault.

What were your first impressions of the patch in general?

I was very surprised that the Facets are gone. I think the issue is that they don’t have a good enough grasp of how to balance certain heroes. So, right now, there’s always a big pool of heroes that are very hard to play and get hit very hard.

A hero like Chen, for example, they’ve been nerfing him for years, but the Facet actually gave him something. So, for Chen to not have his Facet anymore, and what he got in return, you cannot really look at it in the same way. So, it hit this hero really hard, and some other heroes as well.

And some heroes, they just gave five spells. For example, I played Venomancer yesterday. I have five spells. That’s pretty nice. So, they got some work to do.

It’s kind of funny looking at it, because it feels like some of them lost their Facets entirely, and then some of them got both of them. Do you think that’s a bit strange?

It’s interesting, yeah. Maybe they have some favourite heroes. I have no idea. I’m sure they’re trying their best as well. It’s a hard game to balance.

You said the patch didn’t help you in performance. Why do you think that was?

I mean, what was annoying for us was that we were not in shape before the tournament. So, a few days before [the tournament], we started getting in shape, and we showed it during the group stage that we were in pretty decent form.

So, we just learned all these things, and then we were finally finished learning this, so we thought, ‘Okay, nice. Now we just have to learn to execute as well,’ and then everything is gone.

Especially the map movement, because the map change… Like, the hero changes are most interesting for me, and probably for everyone watching, but the map change changes the most, because there’s so much less gold on certain parts of the map.

All of a sudden, your entire movement changes. You need to relearn this entirely. So, that was the hardest, I think, for us.

Do you think there was a bit of a disconnect with how the team is moving throughout the map?

I think we got on the same page when we played our official, but it’s just hard.

We’re not going to do it to the same level that we did before, because the way we practiced moving on the map, we spent a lot of time on it, and we finally felt like we had it figured out. So, that was a bit unfortunate.

The team has struggled for form since PGL Wallachia Season 6. Do you feel like the team wasn’t on top of the previous meta?

Yeah, not sure. I mean, we’re a weird team. We have our own heroes. When we are in shape, I feel like we kind of make our own meta, in a way. Even the last tournament, or this tournament, I mean.

Before the patch dropped, we started experimenting with Nyx mid. I didn’t even know we practiced this, but that’s kind of how we work.

We have a mid player who plays a lot of crazy stuff, and we have our own style on the sidelines. So, when we are in shape, I don’t think the patch matters too much.

It’s more that, if we’re not in shape, we’re very volatile.

In shape in terms of an individual level?

Yeah, it’s mostly individual level, I think, but also as a team. We’re just not that consistent right now. So, it really shows when we’re not feeling it.

One weird aspect of your group stage is that you never had a Bo2 draw. It was all or nothing. Do you think that’s just a random result, or does it show that you need momentum?

There’s probably just some random element to it as well. But when the momentum is in our favour, it really helps us.

I think, before this tournament, I felt we could really win it, but we could also get last. So, it’s tough sometimes.

So it really is just all or nothing, then?

I guess. Hopefully a bit less in the future, but it’s hard to get it under control for now.

With the new patch, what’s your kind of process for getting in shape for the following tournaments?

It’s going to be a weird answer. I didn’t talk too much about it too much yet in public, but I’m probably not going to be playing anymore after this tournament. So for me, it’s not going to be too much.

But I talked to a lot of the guys, and I hope it will be positive for them that there is some change that will help them to figure things out quicker. So it’s a bit random.

Are you moving to another team?

No, it’s just too much time commitment to me, it’s too much stress. Not during a tournament, playing on stage is very fun for me.

But it’s hard to have a life next to Dota as a player. I really enjoy playing still, but it’s better if you don’t have too much of a life outside of Dota.

So it’s… That’s the main reason. I really enjoy playing still. It’s just hard to… It’s better if you don’t have too much of a life outside of Dota.

So you’re retiring then?

You never know, right? Maybe there’s some national tournament, maybe I’ll become a stand-in master.

Who knows? But I’m definitely taking it chill for a while.”

You can discuss the Seleri news on our Discord server!

For more ESL One Interviews, see our chat with ATF about Falcons’ longevity as a roster.

Darragh is an Esports Journalist for Insider Gaming specialising in Counter-Strike. He loves to explore how esports teams work, or why they very often do not.

More from Darragh Harbinson

MOBILE APP

https://insider-gaming.com/seleri-full-interview-esl-one-birmingham/

item.title: Fortnite’s Huge Toy Story Collab Leaks Ahead of Release
item.content:encoded:
Fortnite and Disney have a long-term partnership, including the latter being a major shareholder in Epic Games, and a huge Toy Story collab that has been rumoured for a while has leaked ahead of release.

Toy Story is one of Disney’s most successful franchises, spanning three decades of movies, and the fifth entry in the series of films is released in June, making it the perfect time for an arrival in Fortnite.

While Epic Games has not revealed the full details of the collab or the items that will be available, the April 1 patch uncovered encrypted files that detail what to expect.

Buzz Lightyear, Zurg & Aliens Sidekick Coming to Fortnite

As revealed by the ever-reliable ShiinaBR, Buzz Lightyear and Emperor Zurg are the first two skins to be uncovered for the Toy Story collab, and the pair will first be available through the Buzz vs Zurg Showdown Cup.

Showdown Cups regularly provide early access to skins before they release in the Item Shop, meaning the pair will be available for purchase separately in the future. However, it does mean the chances of a full Toy Story takeover are low.

Bringing Toy Story in as a major collab would certainly have an appeal, bringing super-sized objects into the environment to give off the impression that players are toys, but there is, of course, the issue of the family-friendly theme of Toy Story in amongst a mode with guns, explosives, and more.

This can’t be avoided entirely, and bringing out skins for the likes of Buzz Lightyear will lead to situations where the iconic character is running around with a weapon, but that has become the norm in Fortnite, and can’t be avoided.

Alongside the two revealed skins, it’s also been uncovered that the three-eyed Aliens from the movie are coming as Sidekicks, joining the likes of Spongebob Squarepants and Baby Groot.

Sidekicks were introduced into Fortnite last year and provide a different way for collabs to feature in the game, especially when the size ratio doesn’t quite add up, and the Aliens are the perfect fit for this approach.

What would you like to see Fortnite add to the game in a Toy Story collab? Share your ideas in the Insider Gaming Discord.

For more Fortnite, read about the iconic Marvel villain coming to the game, and check out the exclusive Crimson Desert skin available.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/fortnites-huge-toy-story-collab-leaks-ahead-of-release/

item.title: Another Iconic Marvel Villain is Coming to Fortnite
item.content:encoded:
Fortnite has a long history of collabs with Marvel, including several themed seasons and major live events, and another iconic villain is set to appear in a crossover.

The latest Fortnite OG Season brings the focus back to Marvel, with the return of the Avengers LTM on April 3, and it seems there are no signs of stopping the huge collabs.

Ultron is Coming to Fortnite

According to SamLeakss (via ShiinaBR), Ultron is lined-up as the next major villain set to appear in Fortnite, although the full details have not been disclosed.

Fortnite regularly brings new skins into the game, with the most recent slew of Marvel characters including new looks for Captain America and Iron Man, along with appearances from Luna Snow and Magik from Marvel Rivals.

While Ultron could, in theory, come as a random drop in the shop, he is one of the biggest Marvel villains, and a larger collab would make sense.

Fortnite has had Marvel-themed seasons in the past, with Chapter 2, Season 4 focused on Galactus’ arrival, and Chapter 5, Season 4 putting Doctor Doom as the big bad, and it may be time for another major collab.

It’s a big year for Marvel with the release of Avengers: Doomsday, and Disney will understandably want to promote that huge release. However, as Doom has already featured recently, Ultron could be a good bet.

Ultron was the main villain in the Avengers: Age of Ultron movie, but the one-and-done approach that usually occurs in films does not give the character the justice he deserves, and making him the big bad of an upcoming Fortnite season would see a step forward in giving Ultron more respect.

How do you want to see Ultron introduced to Fortnite? Share your ideas in the Insider Gaming Discord.

For more Fortnite, take a look at the Crimson Desert skin, and read how Ed Sheeran turned down a collab with Fortnite.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

item.title: Is Pokémon Champions Free to Play? Pokémon Champions Price, Explained
item.content:encoded:
Pokémon is going all-out in 2026 to celebrate the franchise’s 30th anniversary, including the release of several games, and the Pokémon Champions price structure has caused a debate.

Fresh from the widely successful release of Pokopia, Pokémon Champions is set to bring a sweep of changes to the competitive scene, making it more accessible than ever.

If you want all the pricing details for Pokémon Champions, Insider Gaming has all the details you need.

Is Pokémon Champions Free to Play?

Yes, Pokémon Champions is free to play, and you won’t have to splash out to download the title on the April 8 launch, or beyond.

Pokémon Champions, like other titles including Pokémon Go, has a base game that is free to play, and you won’t be locked out of specific content entirely if you don’t pay a fee.

  • More Insider Gaming: Pokémon Champions Release Date Countdown & Exact Start Times

Alongside there being no upfront cost, Pokémon Champions does not require an ongoing subscription to play, although you will need a Nintendo Switch Online membership to play online.

While there are no upfront or subscription costs required for Pokémon Champions, there are optional purchases available, including subscriptions and Battle Passes.

Does Pokémon Champions Have Additional Purchases?

Yes, additional purchases are available for Pokémon Champions that can help you fast-track your team, recruit new Pokémon, and more.

For those wanting a jump-start in the game, the Pokémon Champions Starter Pack is available and makes it “easier to both obtain and train Pokémon,” according to Nintendo’s description. This is priced at $9.99.

Additionally, like many other modern titles, Pokémon Champions has a Battle Pass with both a free and a Premium track. For the Premium Battle Pass, it reportedly costs $6.99 a month.

  • More Insider Gaming: Is Pokémon Champions on Nintendo Switch & Mobile?

Finally, there’s also a Pokémon Champions membership that provides bonuses, including additional Pokémon storage, more team slots, exclusive missions and content, and additional bonuses like battle songs. This is expected to cost around $4 a month, or $49.99 a year.

Full details on the payment structure for Pokémon Champions will be revealed when the game releases on April 8.

What are your thoughts on the Pokémon Champions pricing model? Share your thoughts in the Insider Gaming Discord.

For more Pokémon Champions, find out about balance patches that will be used, and check out the first VGC events that will use the game.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/pokemon-champions-free-to-play-price/

item.title: FaZe Secure VRS Lifeline After DracuLan Disaster
item.content:encoded:
FaZe have secured one final chance at qualifying for the IEM Cologne 2026 Counter-Strike Major after the roster ignominiously exited DracuLan Season 6 with a 1-2 record.

The storied roster will now make its way to Belgrade to compete in HLC Belgrade Pro, a 13-team event with a $30,000 prize pool, where they will require LAN wins to prop up their faltering standings in the VRS.

The event will be FaZe’s final opportunity to qualify for the Major before the April 6 VRS cutoff for the IEM Cologne Major invites.

HLC Belgrade Pro details

Date: April 3-5

Prize pool: $30,000

Format: Round-Robin Groups, Playoffs TBD

HLC Belgrade Pro Groups

Group A:

  • FaZe
  • Drama Esports
  • Bee
  • RE’DI

Group B:

  • Illwill
  • Ex-Zero Tenacity
  • Deorum

Group C:

  • BIG
  • Apogee
  • Luminous

Group D:

  • Magic
  • Cypbershoke
  • Pivosquad

Will FaZe magic activate on time?

This wouldn’t be the first time FaZe have come back from the brink to save their Major hopes. The roster survived Stage 1 of the Budapest Major by only 0.437 seconds by preventing Red Canids from defusing on map and series point.

In terms of Major qualification, FaZe required the Last Chance Qualifier at the Paris 2023 Major to make it to the main event.

The LCQ had only been introduced at that event, due to the uneven number of EU spots for that Major, with FaZe taking down Cloud9 to be the last team to make it that time.

So, FaZe are no strangers to completing their homework at the eleventh hour, but fans won’t want to see them play more if they play as they did at DracuLan.

Their performances there were far from ‘The Great Entertainers’ that we have come to know and love. Instead, they looked limp and lost in terms of the map pool as they desperately scrambled to find solutions to their struggling form.

They will also have to do so without a permanent coach, as the org has provided no replacement since removing Filip ‘NEO’ Kubski. Dominik ‘⁠GruBy⁠’ Swiderski remains as the interim coach for the event.

The good news for FaZe is that the field is far weaker at HLC Belgrade Pro, but there will be no room for error in FaZe’s last chance.

Will FaZe make the IEM Cologne 2026 Major? Discuss that on our community Discord server!

For more news, see ESL’s shift to eight-team playoffs and more.

Darragh is an Esports Journalist for Insider Gaming specialising in Counter-Strike. He loves to explore how esports teams work, or why they very often do not.

More from Darragh Harbinson

MOBILE APP

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

item.title: Today’s Contexto: Hints & Answer For April 1, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Contexto is a word association game that gives you a new word to guess every single day. With every attempt, the AI algorithm determines how close your guess is to the daily answer based on thematic and contextual similarity.

Sometimes, though, no matter how experienced you are, the word you’re looking for can be incredibly vague and hard to deduce. Luckily for you, this guide is full of hints to give you a helping hand, but make sure you give today’s Contexto a go if you haven’t already!

How To Play Contexto

Contexto is incredibly simple to play, but difficult to master. All you have to do is type words into the search bar, and the AI algorithm will tell you how close your word is to the answer.

You’ll know how close your guess is based on the colour of the bar and the number next to it.

  • Red includes guesses with the number 1,501 and above, meaning you’re way off from the secret word.
  • Orange includes guesses between 301 and 1,500, meaning you’re getting closer, but still some ways off.
  • Green includes guesses between 1 and 300, meaning you’re on the right track.

Best Contexto Tips & Tricks

  • Start off using broad terms, then narrow down once you’ve got under the 1,000 mark.
  • Try using Nouns first, and dip into verbs or adjectives once you’ve discovered the context.
  • Experiment with random words at times, especially as different forms of a root word can rank differently, such as work and worker.
  • Guess away, as unlike other word games like Wordle, you aren’t limited to a strict number of attempts.

Best Starting Words For Contexto

Here are some great starting words to get you going with Contexto:

  • Thing
  • Person
  • Place
  • Animal
  • Work
  • Nature
  • Game
  • Concept
  • Idea
  • Food

Contexto Hints For Today, April 1, 2026

Without giving away today’s Contexto answer, here are a few helpful hints to get you thinking in the right direction:

  • Hint 1: Ancient practice
  • Hint 2: Affects mind and body
  • Hint 3: Flexibility

If they don’t help, then here are the nine closest words, going from tenth to second:

  • Tantra
  • Massage
  • Exercise
  • Wellness
  • Workout
  • Asana
  • Aerobics
  • Fitness
  • Meditation

Today’s Contexto Answer For April 1, 2026

If you’ve tried everything and still can’t figure out today’s Contexto answer, then you can find it down below:

Answer: Yoga

Yoga is a set of physical and mental exercises, which is intended to give control over the body and mind.

When Is The Next Contexto Game?

Contexto is updated daily at 12 AM or Midnight in your respective time zone, meaning the next game will start on April 2.

The Contexto schedule is very consistent, with a new game releasing at the same time every day.

How did you find today’s Contexto? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more word game guides, check out the following:

  • NYT Mini Crossword

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/todays-contexto-hints-answer-for-april-1-2026/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Is Pokémon Champions on Nintendo Switch & Mobile? All Platforms, Explained
item.content:encoded:
It’s a big year for Pokémon fans with the 30th anniversary of the franchise, and another new title is on the horizon in the form of Pokémon Champions.

Fresh off the back of Pokopia‘s widely successful release, alongside the re-release of Fire Red and Leaf Green, Pokémon is pushing hard in 2026, and Pokémon Champions is set to result in a sweep of changes in the competitive scene.

If you’re interested in the release of Pokémon Champions and want to know what platforms the game is available on, Insider Gaming has all the details.

Is Pokémon Champions on Nintendo Switch?

Yes, Pokémon Champions is available on both generations of the Nintendo Switch console, so those yet to upgrade to a Nintendo Switch 2 will not miss out.

While Nintendo is fully shifting into the Switch 2 era, putting the likes of Pokopia as a next-gen exclusive title, support for the original consoles has not ended completely, and a select few releases will be available on both generations.

  • More Insider Gaming: Pokémon Champions Release Date Countdown & Exact Start Times

Pokémon Champions is among those available on both eras of the console, and cross-generation play means you can face off against someone on a Nintendo Switch 2, regardless of which console you own, and vice versa.

For those on Nintendo Switch 2, an update will be available for Pokémon Champions, rather than a standalone version, providing enhanced visual performance. The update will be free.

Is Pokémon Champions on Mobile?

Pokémon Champions is confirmed to be coming to mobile devices, but the launch will occur later than the Nintendo Switch release on April 8.

A precise release window for Pokémon Champions on mobile has not been announced, but it has been stated that the game will be released on mobile devices at some point in 2026.

  • More Insider Gaming: Pokémon Champions Will Have Balance Patches

This will likely occur in the second half of the year, after the Pokémon World Championships in San Francisco in August, which will give plenty of time to iron out any issues that may occur from the mobile port.

Pokémon Champions is set to become the title that hosts the VGC series of competitive Pokémon, and the availability on mobile devices opens the door to a wider range of players.

Are you excited for the release of Pokémon Champions? Share your thoughts on the game in the Insider Gaming Discord.

For more Pokémon Champions, read about the first VGC events that will use the game, and check out the Battle Pass pricing in Japan.

MOBILE APP
